title: Strikes and lockouts | Employment New Zealand
content:
## Strikes and lockouts
Strikes and lockouts can be used as a tool by parties to collective bargaining or for health and safety reasons. Notice must be given of strikes and lockouts.
- Strikes Employees can legally go on strike if they are members of a union and collective bargaining talks break down, or if they believe there are serious health or safety issues at the workplace.
Employees can legally go on strike if they are members of a union and collective bargaining talks break down, or if they believe there are serious health or safety issues at the workplace.
- Lockouts Lockouts are usually a last resort taken by employers after ongoing industrial action.
Lockouts are usually a last resort taken by employers after ongoing industrial action.
